title: Canva vs Figma
description: Detailed comparison of Canva's accessible graphic design platform versus Figma's professional UI/UX and collaborative design tool.

features.name: Design Systems
features.tool1Detail: Brand kits with colors, fonts, logos
features.tool2Detail: Shared component libraries and design tokens

features.name: Developer Handoff
features.tool2Detail: Inspect mode with CSS, variables, and code export

features.name: AI Features
features.tool1Detail: Magic Design, Magic Write, AI image gen

features.name: Learning Curve
features.tool1Detail: Minutes to learn, no design experience needed

verdict: Canva democratizes design with an incredibly accessible drag-and-drop platform, massive template library, and powerful AI features that enable anyone to create professional-looking graphics, presentations, videos, and print materials in minutes. Figma is the industry standard for professional UI/UX design with advanced prototyping, design systems, developer handoff, and a plugin ecosystem that empowers design teams to build world-class digital products. Marketing teams, social media managers, and non-designers will produce faster results with Canva. Product design teams building interfaces and design systems will find Figma's precision and collaboration unmatched.

faqs.question: Can Figma replace Canva for graphic design?
faqs.answer: Not effectively. Figma is not designed for graphic design tasks like social media graphics, flyers, or presentations. It lacks the template library, AI features, and one-click design tools that make Canva so efficient for marketing content. Most teams use both: Figma for UI/UX and Canva for marketing materials.

faqs.question: Which is better for teams?
faqs.answer: Figma offers superior real-time collaboration with multiplayer editing, live cursors, and branch-based version control that design teams rely on. Canva's team features include comments and approvals but lack the synchronous editing depth. For design teams, Figma wins. For marketing teams creating content together, Canva is sufficient and easier.

faqs.question: Does Canva have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Canva has extensive AI features including Magic Design (generates designs from text prompts), Magic Write (AI copywriting), Magic Eraser, AI image generation, and AI-powered video editing. Figma currently has no built-in AI features, relying on third-party plugins for AI capabilities.

faqs.question: Which is more affordable?
faqs.answer: Both offer free plans. Canva Pro is $12.99/month for a single user, while Figma Professional is $12/editor/month. For teams, Canva Teams ($14.99/user/month for 3+) is slightly cheaper than Figma Organization ($45/editor/month) at scale, though Figma's Professional plan ($12/editor/month) is competitive for smaller teams.

faqs.question: Can I use both Canva and Figma together?
faqs.answer: Yes, they complement each other well. Many teams use Figma for product design and Canva for marketing assets, with assets shared between the two. Both integrate with Slack, Notion, and other collaboration tools, making it easy to maintain a unified design workflow across both platforms.
